seoTitle: How to Unlock a PayPal Account (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to resolve a locked PayPal account by passing security checks, resetting your password, and clearing Resolution Center flags.
intro: A locked account can happen after suspicious login activity, repeated failed passwords, or missing security details. This guide covers the recovery flow from the initial "Trouble Logging In" screen through to a fully restored account. It's for anyone currently unable to access their PayPal login.

steps.heading: Start the recovery flow
steps.body: On the login screen, enter your registered email, and when you see the Account Locked notification, click Trouble Logging In.

steps.heading: Pass the security check
steps.body: Complete the automated CAPTCHA to begin the recovery script.

steps.heading: Choose a verification channel
steps.body: Select either a 6-digit SMS code or a secure email authentication link to verify your identity.

steps.heading: Reset your password
steps.body: Enter the verification code you received, then create a strong new password for your account.

steps.heading: Clear any remaining flags
steps.body: Log in with your new credentials, check the Resolution Center for outstanding checklist items, and submit anything still needed to fully restore access.

faq.question: What usually triggers an automatic account lock?
faq.answer: Unusual login locations, multiple failed password attempts, or missing security answers are common triggers.

faq.question: Do I need to reset my password every time?
faq.answer: Not always, but if your lock was triggered by suspicious login activity, a password reset is often required as part of recovery.

faq.question: Can clearing my browser cookies help?
faq.answer: Yes, clearing cache and using a trusted device can reduce repeated security challenge loops during recovery.

faq.question: How do I know my account is fully unlocked?
faq.answer: Your dashboard will refresh to display a normal status badge instead of any lock or restriction notice.
